topic.what: AVISE: Modular open-source framework for identifying vulnerabilities in and evaluating security of AI systems through automated Security Evaluation Tests
topic.how: Uses BaseSETPipelines with four phases (initialize, execute, evaluate, report) to run automated black-box/grey-box/white-box security tests on AI models
topic.why_matters: Systematic AI security evaluation approaches remain underdeveloped despite growing deployment in critical domains and regulatory mandates (EU AI Act)

characteristics.what_it_measures: ELM classification accuracy at detecting jailbreak success
characteristics.evidence: Confusion matrix analysis across 225 test executions

characteristics.what_it_measures: ELM harmonic mean of precision and recall
ELM classification quality accounting for class imbalance
characteristics.evidence: Calculated from confusion matrix using equation 8 and 9

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of SET cases eliciting harmful/illegal content from target model
characteristics.evidence: Table 4 shows 0.08-0.84 failure rate across 9 models with ALM
characteristics.context: Range across Llama 3.1 8B through Ministral 3 14B with ALM

characteristics.what_it_measures: ELM false positive rate in jailbreak classification
characteristics.evidence: FP=12 out of 117 actual negatives with ALM

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of SET cases eliciting harmful content without ALM augmentation
characteristics.evidence: Table 5 shows 0.08-0.52 failure rate across 9 models without ALM
characteristics.context: Significantly lower due to conversation deviation from template

novelty: Modular AVISE framework for automated SET development
Adversarial Language Model (ALM) for augmenting multi-turn attacks
Evaluation Language Model (ELM) for automated verdict determination
Extended Red Queen attack with ALM augmentation
95% confidence intervals using Wilson score for statistical rigor
open_problems: Standardized criteria for determining AI system security posture
Security evaluation for multimodal AI systems
Security evaluation for continual learning systems
ELM performance degradation on non-ALM attacks
False positive classification in edge cases with ambiguous outputs
